2605.23899.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
2605.23899.paper_summary: Language agents increasingly improve by reusing \emph{skills} -- structured procedural artifacts distilled from past experience. In particular, \emph{domain-level} and \emph{model-generated} skills are especially promising. They offer fast adaptation within a domain by encoding domain-specific recurring procedures, and they scale beyond labor-intensive hand-crafting. However, while extraction methods continue to proliferate, understanding remains limited, with no comprehensive study spanning the full skill lifecycle -- \textbf{experience generation}, \textbf{skill extraction}, and \textbf{skill consumption} -- to ask whether such skills actually work, when they work, and what makes them succeed or fail. To close this gap, we build a utility-grounded evaluation framework that provides systematic experimental results across extractors and target agents, covering five diverse agentic task domains. We find that model-generated skills are beneficial on average but exhibit non-trivial negative transfer, and that neither extractors nor targets behave uniformly. A model can be a strong extractor yet a weak consumer, or vice versa, with skill utility independent of model scale or baseline task strength. To explain these patterns, we then dissect each lifecycle stage in depth, analyzing how experience composition shapes skill quality, what properties characterize useful skills, and how the same skill transfers across different consumers. Finally, we translate these findings into a concrete \emph{meta-skill} that guides skill extraction toward the features tied to actual utility, which consistently improves skill quality across domains and substantially reduces negative transfer.
2605.23899.paper_summary_zh: 語言代理人透過重用\emph{技能} -- 從過去經驗中提煉出的結構化程序工件，逐漸改善。特別是\emph{領域級}和\emph{模型生成}的技能尤其有前景。它們通過編碼特定領域的重複程序，提供了在一個領域內的快速適應，並且超越了勞動密集型的手工製作。然而，儘管提取方法不斷增多，理解仍然有限，沒有一項全面的研究涵蓋整個技能生命周期 -- \textbf{經驗生成}、\textbf{技能提取}和\textbf{技能消耗} -- 來詢問這些技能是否真的有效、何時有效，以及促使其成功或失敗的原因。為了填補這一空白，我們建立了一個基於效用的評估框架，提供了跨提取器和目標代理的系統實驗結果，涵蓋五個不同的代理任務領域。我們發現，模型生成的技能平均上是有益的，但顯示出非平凡的負轉移，並且提取器和目標的行為並不均勻。一個模型可以是強大的提取器卻是弱的消費者，反之亦然，技能的效用與模型的規模或基線任務的強度無關。為了解釋這些模式，我們深入剖析每個生命周期階段，分析經驗組成如何影響技能質量、哪些特性表徵有用的技能，以及相同的技能如何在不同的消費者之間轉移。最後，我們將這些發現轉化為一個具體的\emph{元技能}，引導技能提取朝向與實際效用相關的特徵，這在各個領域中持續改善技能質量並大幅減少負轉移。
2605.23899.author: Zisu Huang et.al.
2605.23899.authors: Zisu Huang, Jingwen Xu, Yifan Yang, Ziyang Gong, Qihao Yang, Muzhao Tian, Xiaohua Wang, Changze Lv, Xuemei Gao, Qi Dai, Bei Liu, Kai Qiu, Xue Yang, Dongdong Chen, Xiaoqing Zheng, Chong Luo
